mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, driving corporate earnings and supporting higher equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS on forward 20-day returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or RSAFS declines while positive returns occur in >60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in the unemployment rate below the 5.0% threshold indicates a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer confidence, leading to positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: More than 50% of triggered instances produce negative 20-day forward returns, or the average return difference between triggered and non-triggered periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is low relative to the consumer price index level, real interest rates compress, reducing discount rates on equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the near term.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC of the FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL ratio on 20-day returns remains below 0.03, or the out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of a long-only signal based on the ratio is negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, decelerating nonfarm payroll growth signals an approaching peak in economic activity, raising recession risk and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tightening labor market
failure_condition: In periods where PAYEMS 3-month growth is below its trailing 12-month average but the economy is in late-cycle (UNRATE < 5%), forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below its 6-month moving average signals a contraction in manufacturing activity, presaging weaker corporate profits and negative equity returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: When INDPRO is below its 6-month MA, more than 50% of 5-day forward returns are positive, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above a high threshold (1500K units) indicate a vigorous residential construction cycle, stimulating broader economic activity and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST > 1500, more than 40% of forward 60-day returns are negative, or the average excess return over non-triggered periods is not positive at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in both retail sales and nonfarm payrolls creates a virtuous cycle of rising consumption and income, reinforcing corporate earnings growth and generating positive equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and PAYEMS are above their respective 12-month moving averages, forward 20-day returns are negative in >30% of instances, or the joint signal's IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a persistently high federal funds rate above 5.0% depresses equity valuations by raising the discount rate and increasing borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 5.0% in a tightening cycle, more than 50% of forward 20-day returns are positive, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).
